summary: The PR claims to be a documentation-only change adding an Installation section to the README, but the diff contains no README changes at all. Instead, it modifies the core slugify logic in index.js and adds a test case, directly contradicting the stated intent.

claims.text: Adds an Installation section to the README covering npm install and yarn add commands
claims.explanation: No README file appears in the diff. The claimed documentation addition is entirely absent.

index.js is modified: the contraction/possessive detection was moved earlier and its regex changed from matching hyphen-delimited patterns (/([a-zA-Z\d]+)-([ts])(-|$)/) to apostrophe-delimited patterns (/([a-zA-Z\d]+)'([ts])(\s|$)/). This is a substantive behavior change, directly contradicting the 'no code or behavior changes' claim.

unstatedChanges.description: Reordered and rewrote the contraction/possessive handling regex, changing it from operating on hyphenated output to operating on raw apostrophes before slug pattern replacement — a real behavioral change to slug generation.
Added a test asserting that 'it-s-hould-not-modify-t-his' is left unchanged, reflecting the behavior change in index.js.
